claims.claim: Fallback texture language is part of Astrid's shadow-self machinery and should not collapse into static canned adjectives.
claims.rationale: Verified existing fallback texture probability and selector tests shift texture terms with telemetry instead of keeping purely static language.

claims.claim: Density_gradient above 0.15 should be able to boost viscous-drag as medium-friction evidence, while preserving sampler and contract authority boundaries.
claims.rationale: Added density-gradient drag boosting with an explicit basis marker and a regression test that asserts the rounded viscous-drag weight rises while the dynamic-bias authority remains diagnostic-only.

claims.claim: Pressure, porosity, mode packing, and silted/viscous resistance should remain visible in fallback texture selection and simulated fallback reviews.
claims.rationale: Ran existing fallback tests for density/packing/friction, silted opacity resistance, pressure-above-0.20 weighted medium, high-pressure capacity review, and settled-foothold viscous suppression.

claims.claim: Gemma4 primary and Ollama fallback profiles should keep model-capacity boundaries visible instead of silently switching or overpromising.
claims.rationale: Verified the high-pressure fallback review keeps the capacity boundary visible without changing model selection or sampler behavior.
